statement: If two continuous maps into a Hausdorff space agree on a dense set, then they are equal.
lean_statement:
theorem continuous_eq_of_eq_on_dense
    {X Y : Type*} [TopologicalSpace X] [TopologicalSpace Y] [T2Space Y]
    (Q : Set X) (hQ : Dense Q) (f g : X → Y)
    (hf : Continuous f) (hg : Continuous g)
    (hfg : ∀ x ∈ Q, f x = g x) :
    f = g
proof:
Proof: let `S = {x | f x = g x}`. In a Hausdorff space, the set `{(y1,y2) | y1 = y2}` is closed in
`Y × Y`, hence `S = (fun x => (f x, g x))⁻¹(diagonal)` is closed in `X` because `f` and `g` are
continuous. By assumption `Q ⊆ S`, and since `Q` is dense we have `closure Q = univ`, so the closed
set `S` containing `Q` must be all of `X`. Therefore `f = g`.
